questions.q: Morarji Desai is noted as the first Prime Minister of India from outside which party?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai was the first Prime Minister not from the Indian National Congress.

questions.q: In which year did Morarji Desai become Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai became Prime Minister in 1977.

questions.q: Morarji Desai headed the government of which party as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai led the Janata Party government as Prime Minister.

questions.q: Morarji Desai is the only Indian to have received the highest civilian honour of which country?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai is the only Indian to have received Pakistan's highest civilian honour.

questions.q: Morarji Desai's term as Prime Minister followed which extraordinary period in Indian politics?
questions.explanation: Desai became Prime Minister after the Emergency, following the 1977 general election.

questions.q: In which Indian state was Morarji Desai born?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai was born in present-day Gujarat.

questions.q: Morarji Desai's becoming Prime Minister in 1977 marked the first change of ruling party at which level?
questions.explanation: His government was the first at the Union level not led by the Congress.

questions.q: Whom did Morarji Desai's party defeat to come to power in the 1977 general election?
options: The Congress led by Indira Gandhi
questions.explanation: The Janata Party defeated the Congress, then led by Indira Gandhi, in the 1977 election.

questions.q: Before becoming Prime Minister, Morarji Desai had served in the Union cabinet in which financial role?
questions.explanation: Desai had earlier served as Union Finance Minister.

questions.q: Morarji Desai became Prime Minister at an advanced age, making him notable as which kind of office-holder?
options: The oldest person to become Prime Minister
questions.explanation: Desai was the oldest person to assume the office of Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: In which year did Morarji Desai's tenure as Prime Minister end?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai's tenure as Prime Minister ended in 1979.

questions.q: Who became Prime Minister immediately after Morarji Desai in 1979?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh succeeded Morarji Desai as Prime Minister in 1979.

questions.q: The 44th Amendment, which revised certain emergency provisions, was enacted during whose tenure as Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment of 1978 was enacted during Morarji Desai's tenure.

questions.q: Before joining national politics, Morarji Desai had served as Chief Minister of which entity?
questions.explanation: Desai served as Chief Minister of the former Bombay State.

questions.q: Morarji Desai had also held which senior position equivalent to the deputy head of government under Indira Gandhi before the Congress split?
questions.explanation: Desai served as Deputy Prime Minister in the Union government before later leading the Janata Party.

questions.q: Morarji Desai was awarded India's highest civilian honour, the Bharat Ratna, in which year?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 1991.

questions.q: Morarji Desai began his career in public service in which profession before entering politics?
questions.explanation: Desai began as a civil servant before joining the freedom movement and politics.

questions.q: The Janata Party that Morarji Desai led was formed by the merger of several parties opposed to which group?
options: The ruling Congress of that time
questions.explanation: The Janata Party was formed by merging several parties opposed to the then-ruling Congress.

questions.q: Morarji Desai presented the Union Budget several times. In which capacity did he do so?
questions.explanation: Desai presented several Union Budgets during his tenures as Finance Minister.

questions.q: Morarji Desai is associated with which personal lifestyle and disciplinary commitment widely noted in public life?
questions.explanation: Desai was widely noted for his strict personal discipline and austere habits.

questions.q: The 1977 general election that brought Desai to power followed the lifting of which measure?
questions.explanation: The 1977 election was held after the Emergency was lifted in March 1977.

questions.q: Morarji Desai is associated with a policy of prohibition concerning which of the following?
questions.explanation: Desai was a longstanding advocate of prohibition of alcohol.

questions.q: In which year was Morarji Desai born?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai was born in 1896.

questions.q: Morarji Desai was the oldest person to take office as Prime Minister, doing so at approximately what age?
questions.explanation: Desai became Prime Minister in 1977 at around the age of 81.

questions.q: Pakistan's highest civilian honour conferred on Morarji Desai is named which of the following?
questions.explanation: Desai was conferred the Nishan-e-Pakistan, Pakistan's highest civilian award.

questions.q: Morarji Desai lived to a notably advanced age, passing away in which year?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai died in 1995, at around the age of 99.

questions.q: Approximately how long did Morarji Desai's single tenure as Prime Minister last?
options: About two years and four months
questions.explanation: His tenure ran from March 1977 to July 1979, roughly two years and four months.

questions.q: Morarji Desai's government took office after the Janata Party's victory, in which the constituent groups had united largely in response to what?
questions.explanation: The Janata Party coalesced largely in reaction to the experience of the Emergency.

questions.q: Before independence, Morarji Desai participated in which broad national effort?
options: The freedom movement against colonial rule
The drafting of the Reserve Bank Act
The founding of the United Nations
questions.explanation: Desai was a participant in the freedom movement against colonial rule before independence.

questions.q: Morarji Desai's ending of his premiership in 1979 was due primarily to which immediate cause?
options: Completion of a full five-year term
Loss of majority support in the Lok Sabha
A constitutional amendment abolishing the office
questions.explanation: Desai's government fell when it lost majority support in the Lok Sabha in 1979.
